title: TrailGenic Overextension Fasted Hiking Safety Dataset
description: Shell dataset module modeling physiological risk factors and safety considerations when endurance activity is performed in a fasted metabolic state for extended durations.

records.mechanism: Hypoglycemia risk during prolonged fasted endurance activity
physiological_response: increased reliance on hepatic glucose production
adaptation_outcomes: development of pacing discipline during fasted endurance activity
improved metabolic resilience when training stress is managed appropriately

supporting_science.finding: Prolonged endurance activity without adequate carbohydrate availability increases risk of hypoglycemia and fatigue.

records.mechanism: Excessive cortisol and stress response from prolonged energy deficit
adaptation_outcomes: recognition of safe duration limits for fasted endurance activity
improved balance between metabolic stress and recovery

supporting_science.finding: Prolonged endurance exercise can elevate cortisol levels and increase physiological stress responses.

records.mechanism: Dehydration and electrolyte imbalance during fasted endurance exposure
adaptation_outcomes: integration of electrolyte strategies during fasted endurance training
greater physiological stability during long-duration efforts

supporting_science.finding: Fluid and electrolyte balance are critical for maintaining physiological stability during prolonged endurance exercise.

records.mechanism: Overtraining risk from repeated high-duration fasted endurance sessions
adaptation_outcomes: development of sustainable endurance training strategies

supporting_science.finding: Insufficient recovery between endurance sessions can lead to overtraining syndrome and impaired physiological adaptation.
